title: China narrows the AI gap with the US to a record-low 6%
excerpt: The gap between American and Chinese AI is closing fast. Chinese models narrowed the performance shortfall to a record-low 6% in June, down from 9% in May, according to Bloomberg Intelligence. The research arm said the shift raises real questions about how long US supremacy in AI will hold. The lead used to look comfortable. […]
content_html:
The gap between American and Chinese AI is closing fast. Chinese models narrowed the performance shortfall to a record-low 6% in June, down from 9% in May, according to Bloomberg Intelligence. The research arm said the shift raises real questions about how long US supremacy in AI will hold.

The lead used to look comfortable. Over the previous 12 months, the gap averaged 10% to 15%, BI said. Then Zhipu AI’s GLM-5.2 reached the top of the global ranking in agentic coding, and the maths changed.

It was not a solo act. Chinese models took two slots in LiveBench’s global ranking of large language model performance last month. The arrival of Moonshot’s Kimi “highlights that Zhipu’s success was not a one-off,” said Robert Lea, a Bloomberg Intelligence analyst, in a report.

Two philosophies, one race

The two sides are not playing the same game. American labs lean on plentiful Nvidia chips to push performance as far as it will go, BI said, and pay a heavy compute bill for it. Chinese firms take a leaner, more efficient path. They may trade away some performance to do so.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

Which wins is still an open question. “Time will tell which approach yields greater results,” the analysts wrote. For now, the efficient approach is closing the distance quickly.

Why the number lands hard

The timing gives the figure weight. A cheap Chinese model has spent weeks unsettling US investors and officials. Kimi K3’s launch set off a sharp market reaction, echoing DeepSeek a year earlier. US officials have since floated sanctions over alleged model theft.

A single percentage point will not settle a technology race. But the direction is clear. The story has moved from whether China can keep pace to how small the gap can get.

content_html_zh:
美国和中国人工智能之间的差距正在迅速缩小。 据 Bloomberg Intelligence 称，6 月份中国模型的业绩差距从 5 月份的 9% 缩小至历史最低的 6%。 该研究机构表示，这一转变引发了关于美国在人工智能领域的霸主地位能维持多久的真正问题。

铅曾经看起来很舒服。 BI 表示，在过去 12 个月中，这一差距平均为 10% 至 15%。 随后，智浦AI的GLM-5.2在代理编码方面登上了全球榜首，数学发生了变化。

这不是一个独奏表演。 上个月，中国模型在 LiveBench 全球大语言模型性能排名中占据了两个位置。 彭博资讯 (Bloomberg Intelligence) 分析师罗伯特·利 (Robert Lea) 在一份报告中表示，Moonshot 的 Kimi 的到来“凸显了智浦的成功并非一次性”。

两种哲学，一种种族

双方打的不是同一场比赛。 BI 表示，美国实验室依靠大量的 Nvidia 芯片来最大限度地提高性能，并为此支付了高昂的计算费用。 中国企业走的是一条更精简、更高效的道路。 为此，他们可能会牺牲一些性能。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

谁获胜仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。 分析师写道：“时间会证明哪种方法会产生更好的结果。” 目前，有效的方法是迅速拉近距离。

为什么这个数字很难落地

时间赋予了人物重量。 廉价的中国模式已经持续数周让美国投资者和官员感到不安。 Kimi K3的推出引起了激烈的市场反应，与一年前的DeepSeek相呼应。 此后，美国官员对涉嫌盗窃模型的行为实施了制裁。

一个百分点并不能解决技术竞赛。 但方向是明确的。 故事的焦点从中国是否能跟上步伐，转向了差距能缩小到多大。
